title: auto should be avoided to deduce raw pointers
description: "auto" should not be used to deduce raw pointersAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode Smellconfusingsince-c++11Why is this an issue?Usingautowhen the type that would be deduced is a pointer type can cause confusion. It is much better to specify the pointer part outside ofauto.Noncompliant code exampleauto item = new Item(); // NoncompliantCompliant solutionauto* item = new Item();Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: auto item = new Item(); // Noncompliant: pointer type not explicit
auto ptr = malloc(100); // Noncompliant: unclear that it's a pointer
compliant: auto* item = new Item(); // Compliant: explicit pointer declaration
auto* ptr = static_cast<char*>(malloc(100)); // Compliant: clear pointer type
Item* item = new Item(); // Compliant: explicit type

checks.message: auto should be avoided to deduce raw pointers - use explicit pointer type or auto* instead.
